question.text: When a trader receives a Mark-to-Market (MTM) profit on their futures position, the accounting entry is:

answer.text: Debit: Broker/Margin Account; Credit: Profit on Futures Trading Account
Debit: Cash Account; Credit: Capital Account
Debit: Initial Margin Account; Credit: Bank Account
No entry is required until the position is closed

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** MTM profits must be recognized daily as they represent realized gains (settled in cash). Entry: Debit: Broker/Margin Account (Asset increases as money is received) Credit: Profit on Futures Trading Account (Income recognition)
For MTM loss: Debit: Loss on Futures Trading Account; Credit: Broker/Margin Account
**The Trap:** MTM is a DAILY settlement - it must be recorded daily, not only at position close. The profit/loss is realized, not notional.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Daily MTM accounting is a unique feature of derivatives accounting.
**Key Takeaway:** MTM profit: Debit Margin, Credit P&L. MTM loss: Debit P&L, Credit Margin. Record daily.
